message:
fix(messaging/routing): reject source patterns without literal anchor segment

Routing programs with prefix-rewrite semantics require a source pattern
that starts with a literal segment (e.g. "orders.created.*") so the
router can match and strip a known prefix before applying the rewrite.
A wildcard-leading pattern like "*.orders.created" has no stable prefix
to match against, making the rewrite semantics undefined.

Add ensure_literal_source_anchor() validation during compile_export()
that fails closed with a new NonLiteralSourcePatternAnchor error when
the first segment is a wildcard (* or >) instead of a literal.

Previously, such patterns would compile successfully and produce
incorrect routing behavior at runtime — silently matching the wrong
subjects or failing to strip the intended prefix.

Adds test verifying that "*.orders.created" is rejected at compile time
with the correct error variant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
